title: Level 443
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 443: "Trap Compression Corridors System"}}{{Liminal Theme}}{{Blurbackground|file=LigneousOpulence.jpg}} {{Class 1|color-h=50|color-s=20%|class=3|hexagon=3|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|entity=Devoid of Entities}} '''Level 443''' is the 444th level of the Backrooms. ==Ⅰ. Overview== ===General=== {{Blurimage|file=LigneousOpulence.jpg|caption=An image of the level took by [[Entity_1|The Cameraman]] in a ''Standard Corridor''.|align=right}} '''Level 443''', also known as ''"Trap Compression Corridors System"'', manifests as an extensive and spatially continuous network of curvilinear corridors. The environment presents repetitive architectural patterns. The walls reach significant height and are composed of various materials, most frequently wood or concrete. Their coloration is not fixed; documented tones include brown, gray, and desaturated variations of pastel colors, with gradual transitions observable over extended distances. The floor and ceiling are uniformly constructed from polished white marble. Lighting across the level is irregular. Some areas contain low-intensity yellow or white illumination, while others exhibit high-intensity lighting. Multiple sectors lack illumination entirely, resulting in prolonged darkness. Transitions between lighting states occur abruptly. The corridors generally lack furnishing, although specific locations contain support pillars, inactive surveillance devices, and nonfunctional ventilation grilles. Wall trims throughout the level possess consistently sharp edges capable of producing severe lacerations upon limited contact. Moisture is present in several sectors. Certain surfaces present a continuous leak of a dark, unidentified fluid from both walls and ceilings. Other areas are coated in a viscous adhesive substance, proven to be unsafe for contact or ingestion.Analysis revealed that both the liquid and the substance coating from certain areas of the level contain a chemical composition consisting of a mixture of ''cerebrospinal fluid,'' ''amniotic fluid,'' and [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]. ===
Corridor Variants
=== {{Blurimage|file=Anotherimageformynewlevel.jpg|align=left|caption=An image of a ''Luxury Corridor''.}} ''Standard Corridors'' constitute the majority of '''Level 443'''´s structure. These areas maintain the curvilinear layout, variable wall coloration, and inconsistent illumination patterns described above. They do not typically contain additional structural elements beyond isolated pillars or inactive devices. In addition to the previously provided information, the audio recording analysis in these corridors detects low-frequency pulsations distributed evenly through the environment; the origin of these pulsations remains undetermined. ''Luxury Corridors'' are distinguished by the presence of integrated neon lighting installed along upper wall segments. The neon lighting increases visual clarity but periodically exhibits irregular fluctuations in intensity. Despite the modified illumination, these corridors retain all structural and environmental characteristics of the standard layout, along with the previously mentioned low-frequency pulsations.Analysis revealed that the low-frequency pulsations could be translated into Morse code. However, they could only reproduce random sequences of letters. ''Decayed Corridors'' are rare sectors in which walls, floors, or ceilings exhibit significant degradation, including cracking and partial loss of surface material. These areas expose an internal layer composed of a white, rigid substance with structural properties similar to a mix between flesh and human bone. This material demonstrates high resistance and has only been damaged through the explosions of a large amount of [[Object 15|Fire Salt]], during a test. It is unknown what originally caused the damage in some of the level´s corridors. There are also several minor variants that are of lesser significance and, therefore, do not require a detailed examination. These include, for example, corridors with inclined walls, corridors with flat walls, and corridors with more pronounced wall trims, among others. ==Ⅱ. Properties== The level has three documented states: the ''Neutral State'', the ''Danger State'', and the ''Attack State''. Under ordinary circumstances, the level remains in the ''Neutral State'', staying at full structural stability, steady illumination, and an absence of any unusual activity. The ''Danger State'' is precipitated by acute external stimuli, including forceful impacts, abrupt directional shifts, or high-intensity impulsive sounds. Once triggered, the entire level enters a phase of persistent low-frequency tremors that spread uniformly through all corridors. Additional events include sudden light failures and atypical auditory events originating from within or behind the walls, suggesting internal mechanical stress or environmental agitation. If loud auditory disturbances or sustained movement patterns persist during the ''Danger State'', the level may escalate into the ''Attack State'', the most hazardous state observed. In this phase, the tremors intensify. Simultaneously, the walls close to constrict at uneven rates, progressively narrowing the corridors and generating rapidly diminishing spaces. All lighting systems shut down, leaving the environment in total darkness. After all wanderers on the level are crushed during this event, the level initiates an undocumented restorative process and subsequently returns to its ''Neutral State''. The [[M.E.G.]] has conducted multiple tests to document these properties.
DOCUMENTED TESTS:
''Sensitivity Test:''

A series of controlled experiments to determine the responsiveness of '''Level 443''' to external stimuli was conducted. Loud noises, rapid movements, and forceful impacts were systematically applied in various corridors while observing structural, environmental, and lighting changes. Results indicated that the level consistently transitioned from its ''Neutral State'' into ''Danger State'' following the application of these stimuli. The intensity and duration of the response correlated with the magnitude and repetition of the triggers. In multiple instances, prolonged or repeated stimuli escalated the environment into ''Attack State'', confirming that the level reacts to cumulative provocations. Observations suggest that the level possesses a form of perceptual awareness or instinctive response mechanism. While no evidence of higher cognition was documented, the consistent, predictable reactions to specific triggers indicate that the environment actively monitors and responds to external disturbances.
''Explosion Stress Test:''

A substantial quantity of [[Object_15|Firesalt]] was deployed within '''Level 443''' to neutralize its anomalous properties. The instances were positioned in a central section of the corridor system and detonated safely. Following the detonation, '''Level 443''' entered a continuous ''Danger State'' lasting approximately three months. During this period, the environment displayed persistent low-frequency vibrations, irregular lighting patterns, and aggressive constriction of corridors. No external intervention was capable of restoring the level to a ''Neutral State'' during this interval. After three months, the level completed a full structural regeneration process. The gap created by the explosion was eliminated, and the level returned to its ''Neutral State''.
==Ⅲ. Discovery== {{Blurimage|caption=Early-state image of the level, attached to a project file within a terminal.|file=AnotherImageForMyNewLevel.jpg}} On January 24th, 2023, a sealed maintenance door was found in a corridor of [[Level 15]]. The door required an access card, but due to structural deterioration, it was ultimately forced open using improvised tools. Inside, there was a control chamber containing nonfunctional and heavily damaged technology, along with several papers, plans, and terminals. An active terminal was found, which had a file that explained a project designated as the ''Trap Compression Corridors System'', described as an artificial biomechanical level constructed with an extended series of corridors throughout hidden areas of [[Level 15]]. After analyzing the documents, [[M.E.G.]] researchers came up with the theory that the project was intended as a remotely operated weapon during the [[Backroom Colonists|Bloodbath of Cluster 1]], designed to lure opposing factions into a seemingly safe level before crushing everyone inside it. The project appears to have been cancelled early in its development, likely due to the level exhibiting unpredictable autonomous reactions. The cause of its self-awareness remains unknown, and no known faction claims responsibility for its development. The surviving data recovered from the control chamber was transmitted to the [[M.E.G.]] through [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]] by the individuals who first accessed the site and documented its interior. Later revisions on the chamber’s terminals made it possible for the [[M.E.G.]] to collect and verify additional archival records. These documents included a map to the ''TCCS''. An exploration team was deployed to locate and map the ''TCCS'', unaware of the risks it housed. The team was declared missing on March 3rd, 2023, after all communication ceased without warning. In response, the [[M.E.G.]] temporarily sealed every known access point to the level as a preventative measure. The entrance remained closed until January 21st, 2026, when various petitionsSome [[M.E.G.]] researchers hypothesized that the level might contain numerous significant concealed elements or technology. resulted in its controlled reopening for limited research purposes. ==Ⅳ. Entities== Entities such as [[Entity 63|Predatory Doors]] and [[Entity_144|Filing Cabinets]] used to inhabit areas near the exits. However, it is believed they went extinct, as the last documented encounter with one was on February 14th, 2026. ==Ⅴ. Bases, Colonies & Outposts== No bases, colonies, or outposts could be established within the level for obvious reasons. Instead, warning signs were placed at the level’s entrances to prevent wanderers from entering the level. ==Ⅵ. Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== *A series of hallways hidden in a secret chamber labelled ''"Project TCCS"'' in [[Level 15]], [[Level 464]], or [[Level 754]] will take one here. ===Exits=== *Traveling the entire level will bring one back to [[Level 15]], [[Level 464]], or [[Level 754]]. ==Ⅶ.
